report_name: Okta Customers Targeted by Large-Scale Credential Stuffing Campaign

attack_patterns.name: Credential Stuffing
attack_patterns.description: The core technique used by attackers, leveraging massive lists of previously breached usernames and passwords to automate login attempts against Okta accounts.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The ultimate goal of the credential stuffing attack is to gain access to valid user accounts on the Okta platform, which can then be used for unauthorized access to connected applications.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: Once an Okta account is compromised, attackers leverage the trusted relationship between Okta and its connected applications (like Salesforce, Office 365) to gain unauthorized access to corporate resources.

tools.name: Tor
tools.description: Used by attackers to anonymize their traffic and bypass IP-based blocking when conducting credential stuffing attacks against Okta.

tools.name: OpenBullet
tools.description: Mentioned as an example of a credential stuffing tool used to automate login attempts against Okta's authentication endpoints.

tools.name: SNIPR
tools.description: Mentioned as an example of a credential stuffing tool used to automate login attempts against Okta's authentication endpoints.

identities.name: Okta
identities.description: Identity and access management provider whose platform and customers were targeted by a large-scale credential stuffing campaign. Okta issued the warning about the attacks.

identities.name: Okta Customers
identities.description: The primary targets of the credential stuffing campaign, whose user accounts were at risk of takeover.

infrastructure.name: Tor Anonymization Network
infrastructure.description: A distributed network of relays used by attackers to obscure the origin of their credential stuffing traffic against Okta customers.

infrastructure.name: Commercial Proxy Services
infrastructure.description: Various commercial proxy services used alongside Tor to distribute the attack traffic and evade IP-based detection during the credential stuffing campaign.

indicators.name: Okta High Volume of Credential Stuffing Attempts
indicators.pattern:
title: Okta High Volume of Failed Logins
id: 2b1a9b3d-8e4f-4f8a-9d6c-7c8d9e0f1a2b
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects a high volume of failed login attempts for a single user account from multiple source IP addresses, which is indicative of a credential stuffing attack.
  This rule is based on hunting hints provided in a report about attacks targeting Okta customers.
references:
  - https://www.cognisys.com/blog/the-biggest-cyber-attacks-and-vulnerabilities-of-april-2024/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2024-04-27
modified: 2024-04-27
tags:
  - attack.t1110.003
  - attack.credential_access
logsource:
  product: okta
  service: okta
detection:
  selection:
    eventtype: 'user.session.start'
    outcome.result: 'FAILURE'
  condition: selection | count(distinct client.ip_address) by actor.alternateId > 10
falsepositives:
  - A user attempting to log in from multiple devices or locations in a short period of time, though the high threshold should minimize this.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects a high volume of failed Okta login events for a single user from numerous different IP addresses, a strong indicator of a credential stuffing attack.

indicators.name: Okta Login from Anonymizing Service
indicators.pattern:
title: Okta Login From Anonymizing Service
id: 8c5d6e7f-1a2b-4c8d-9e0f-1a2b3c4d5e6f
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects successful Okta logins originating from known anonymizing services such as Tor exit nodes or commercial proxies. Such activity is highly suspicious and could indicate a compromised account.
references:
  - https://www.cognisys.com/blog/the-biggest-cyber-attacks-and-vulnerabilities-of-april-2024/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2024-04-27
modified: 2024-04-27
tags:
  - attack.t1078
  - attack.initial_access
logsource:
  product: okta
  service: okta
detection:
  selection:
    eventtype: 'user.session.start'
    outcome.result: 'SUCCESS'
  filter:
    client.ip_address:
      # This list should be populated with known Tor exit nodes and commercial proxy IPs
      - '1.2.3.4' 
      - '5.6.7.8'
  condition: selection and filter
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate users using VPNs or privacy services that overlap with known proxy IP ranges.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects successful logins to Okta from IP addresses associated with known anonymizing services like Tor or commercial proxies, as mentioned in the attack analysis.

relationships.source_name: Okta High Volume of Credential Stuffing Attempts
